audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for mercy/service, veiled gospel, light in hearts, treasure in clay vessels, death/life of Jesus, outer wasting/inner renewal, seen/unseen.

verses.restored: Because of this, having this service/ministry, just as we were shown mercy, we do not lose heart.

But we renounced the hidden things of shame, not walking in craftiness nor falsifying the word of the Divine, but by manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every human conscience before the Divine.
And if our good news is veiled, it is veiled among those perishing,
in whom the god of this age blinded the thoughts of the untrusting, so that the illumination of the good news of the glory of Messiah, who is image of the Divine, might not shine.
For we do not proclaim ourselves, but Jesus Messiah as Lord, and ourselves as your servants because of Jesus.
Because the Divine who said, Out of darkness light will shine, is the One who shone in our hearts for illumination of the knowledge of the glory of the Divine in the face of Jesus Messiah.
But we have this treasure in clay vessels, so that the surpassing power may be of the Divine and not from us.
In every way pressured, but not crushed; perplexed, but not despairing;
pursued/persecuted, but not abandoned; struck down, but not destroyed;
always carrying around in the body the dying of Jesus, so that the life of Jesus may also be made manifest in our body.
For we who live are always being handed over to death because of Jesus, so that the life of Jesus also may be made manifest in our mortal flesh.
So then death works in us, but life in you.
Having the same spirit of trust according to what has been written, I trusted, therefore I spoke, we also trust, therefore we also speak,
knowing that the One who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus and present us with you.
For all things are because of you, so that grace, having increased through the many, may cause thanksgiving to abound into the glory of the Divine.
Therefore we do not lose heart; but even if our outer human is being decayed, yet our inner human is being renewed day by day.
For the momentary lightness of our pressure is working for us an age-long weight of glory beyond measure upon measure,
while we do not look at the things seen, but the things not seen; for the things seen are temporary, but the things not seen are age-long.

translation_notes: Treasure in clay vessels is not body-hatred; the vulnerable body becomes the site where Divine power is made visible.
Outer decay and inner renewal should be held together with resurrection hope, not used to despise embodiment.
